textPlain: Why do they keep making them BIGGER and BIGGER? Our hands don't grow that fast, most adult males have been struggling using their phone with one hand. Only the vocal minority prefers to oversized phone-computer, most of us just want to use it briefly on the go before tucking it back into the pocket, without it tearing a hole in it (which my last two phones have done).If anyone is listening -- can you put a cap on the dimensions? 5.5" screen is plenty, if I want the cinema experience I will either a) go to cinema or b) use some VR/AR device, for the rest of use cases, like watching a movie on a bus/plane/train, it doesn't weigh up against carrying a brick with you.
New Linux phone dropsLooks insideStill the good old A76 and A55 cores (they're 8 years old at this point)
the FuriPhone FLX1 was  A Debian-powered brick that puts GNOME in your back pocketprevious discussion https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=41839326
Looks interesting. Posh is a bit too adventurous for me (I wish there was a smartphone running FOSS android out of the box)
The website is down from all the visitors at the moment.Anyone here can share their experience with the phone?
Expecting a design lawsuit from Apple
And can you give a number on the "vocal minority"? Because companies usually sell what customers want and if the majority of the phones on the market is big, then that's what people want.
I like large screens because I value having plenty of context visible, e.g. in a webpage or a conversation.Also, don't forget the bigger batteries that large phones enable.
Do smaller phones still exist?Genuinely asking. I’m on iPhone, which hasn’t changed form factor in quite a while.
As much as I admire the FOSS nature, it's always the problem of underspeccing and overpricing the tech at the same time.

how does that happen btw? like it's understandable when a website is hosted on a vape (lol), but even a cheap vps should be able to handle like 10-20k views in the span of a couple hours (which is the max load from HN i'm assuming), unless you're hosting video or some such
